title: Anorectal pain documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.
red_flags.rationale: Supports defecation-related pain, bleeding and bowel context for anorectal pain documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Pain phenotype, perianal infection, fistula, trauma, inflammatory disease, mass, full examination, diagnosis, investigation, treatment and urgency remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for anorectal pain documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
